sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
A[G]mazing [G7]grace, how [C]sweet the [G]sound,
That saved a wretch like [Em]me![D]
I [G]once was [G7]lost, but [C]now I'm [G]found,
Was blind, but [D7]now I [G]see.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
'Twas [G]grace that [G7]taught my [C]heart to [G]fear,
And grace my fears re[Em]lieved.[D]
How [G]precious [G7]did that [C]grace ap[G]pear
The hour I [D7]first be[G]lieved.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
Through [G]many [G7]dangers, [C]toils and [G]snares,
I have al[Em]ready [D]come.
'Tis [G]grace hath [G7]brought me [C]safe thus [G]far,
And grace will [D7]lead me [G]home.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.content:
The [G]Lord hath [G7]promised [C]good to [G]me,
His word my hope se[Em]cures.[D]
He [G]will my [G7]shield and [C]portion [G]be
As long as [D7]life en[G]dures.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.content:
When [G]we've been [G7]there ten [C]thousand [G]years,
Bright shining as the [Em]sun,[D]
We've [G]no less [G7]days to [C]sing God's [G]praise
Than when we [D7]first be[G]gun.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
O [G]Lord my [C]God, when I in [G]awesome wonder
Consider [Am]all the [D]worlds Thy hands have [G]made,
I see the [C]stars, I hear the [G]rolling thunder,
Thy power through[Am]out the [D]universe dis[G]played.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.content:
Then sings my [G]soul, my [G7]Savior God, to [C]Thee:
How great Thou [G]art! How great Thou [D]art!
Then sings my [G]soul, my [G7]Savior God, to [C]Thee:
How great Thou [G]art! [D]How great Thou [G]art!

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
When [G]through the [C]woods and forest [G]glades I wander
And hear the [Am]birds sing [D]sweetly in the [G]trees,
When I look [C]down from lofty [G]mountain grandeur
And hear the [Am]brook and [D]feel the gentle [G]breeze.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
And [G]when I [C]think that God, His [G]Son not sparing,
Sent Him to [Am]die, I [D]scarce can take it [G]in,
That on the [C]cross, my burden [G]gladly bearing,
He bled and [Am]died to [D]take away my [G]sin.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.content:
When [G]Christ shall [C]come with shout of [G]acclamation
And take me [Am]home, what [D]joy shall fill my [G]heart!
Then I shall [C]bow in humble [G]adoration
And there pro[Am]claim, my [D]God, how great Thou [G]art!

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
[D]Holy, holy, [G]holy! [D]Lord God Al[A]mighty!
[D]Early in the [G]morning our [A]song shall rise to [D]Thee.
[D]Holy, holy, [G]holy! [D]Merciful and [Bm]mighty!
[G]God in three [D]Persons, [A]blessed Trini[D]ty!

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
[D]Holy, holy, [G]holy! [D]All the saints a[A]dore Thee,
[D]Casting down their [G]golden crowns a[A]round the glassy [D]sea;
[D]Cherubim and [G]seraphim [D]falling down be[Bm]fore Thee,
[G]Which wert and [D]art, and [A]evermore shalt [D]be.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
[D]Holy, holy, [G]holy! [D]Though the darkness [A]hide Thee,
[D]Though the eye of [G]sinful man Thy [A]glory may not [D]see.
[D]Only Thou art [G]holy; [D]there is none be[Bm]side Thee
[G]Perfect in [D]power, in [A]love, and puri[D]ty.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.content:
[D]Holy, holy, [G]holy! [D]Lord God Al[A]mighty!
[D]All Thy works shall [G]praise Thy name in [A]earth and sky and [D]sea.
[D]Holy, holy, [G]holy! [D]Merciful and [Bm]mighty!
[G]God in three [D]Persons, [A]blessed Trini[D]ty!

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
[D]Great is Thy [G]faithfulness, [D]O God my [A]Father,
[D]There is no [G]shadow of [A]turning with [D]Thee.
[D]Thou changest [G]not, Thy com[Em]passions they [A]fail not;
[G]As Thou hast [D]been Thou for[A]ever wilt [D]be.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.content:
[D]Great is Thy [G]faithfulness! [D]Great is Thy [A]faithfulness!
[D]Morning by [Bm]morning new [Em]mercies I [A]see.
[D]All I have [G]needed Thy [Em]hand hath pro[A]vided;
[G]Great is Thy [D]faithfulness, [A]Lord unto [D]me.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
[D]Summer and [G]winter, and [D]springtime and [A]harvest,
[D]Sun, moon and [G]stars in their [A]courses a[D]bove,
[D]Join with all [G]nature in [Em]manifold [A]witness
[G]To Thy great [D]faithfulness, [A]mercy and [D]love.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
[D]Pardon for [G]sin and a [D]peace that en[A]dureth,
[D]Thy own dear [G]presence to [A]cheer and to [D]guide.
[D]Strength for to[G]day and bright [Em]hope for to[A]morrow,
[G]Blessings all [D]mine, with ten [A]thousand be[D]side!

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
To [G]God be the glory, great [D]things He has [G]done!
So [C]loved He the [G]world that He [A7]gave us His [D]Son,
Who [G]yielded His life an a[D]tonement for [G]sin,
And [C]opened the [G]life-gate that [D7]all may go [G]in.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.content:
[G]Praise the Lord, [G7]praise the Lord, let the [C]earth hear His [G]voice!
Praise the Lord, praise the Lord, let the [A7]people re[D]joice!
O [G]come to the Father through [C]Jesus the [G]Son,
And [C]give Him the [G]glory, great [D7]things He has [G]done.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
O [G]perfect redemption, the [D]purchase of [G]blood,
To [C]every be[G]liever the [A7]promise of [D]God;
The [G]vilest offender who [D]truly be[G]lieves,
That [C]moment from [G]Jesus a [D7]pardon re[G]ceives.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
Great [G]things He has taught us, great [D]things He has [G]done,
And [C]great our re[G]joicing through [A7]Jesus the [D]Son;
But [G]purer, and higher, and [D]greater will [G]be
Our [C]wonder, our [G]transport, when [D7]Jesus we [G]see.

songs.title: A Mighty Fortress Is Our God
songs.author: Martin Luther

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
A [C]mighty [G]fortress [Am]is our [D7]God,
A [Am]bulwark [Em]never [F]fail[C]ing;[Am][Dm][G7][C]
Our [G]helper [Am]He a[D7]mid the [G]flood
Of [Am]mortal [Em]ills pre[F]vail[C]ing.[Am][Dm][G7][C]
For [Am]still our [G]ancient [D7]foe
Doth [C]seek to [G7]work us [C]woe;[F][E][Am]
His [E]craft and [Am]pow'r are [G]great,[C][D7][G]
And [Dm]armed with [F]cruel [C]hate,[A][Dm][E]
On [Am]earth is [G7]not his [C]equal.[A][Dm][G7][C]

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
Did [C]we in [G]our own [Am]strength con[D7]fide,
Our [Am]striving [Em]would be [F]los[C]ing,[Dm][G7][C]
Were [G]not the [Am]right man [D7]on our [G]side,
The [Am]man of [Em]God's own [F]choos[C]ing.[Dm][G7][C]
Dost [Am]ask who [G]that may [D7]be?
[C]Christ [G7]Jesus, [C]it is [F]He;[E][Am]
[E]Lord [Am]Sabaoth [G]His [C]name,[D7][G]
From [Dm]age to [F]age the [C]same,[A][Dm][E]
And [Am]He must [G7]win the [C]battle.[Dm][G7][C]

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
And [C]though this [G]world with [Am]devils [D7]filled,
Should [Am]threaten [Em]to un[F]do [C]us,[Dm][G7][C]
We [G]will not [Am]fear, for [D7]God hath [G]willed
His [Am]truth to [Em]triumph [F]through [C]us.[Dm][G7][C]
The [Am]prince of [G]darkness [D7]grim,
We [C]tremble [G7]not for [C]him;[F][E][Am]
His [E]rage we [Am]can en[G]dure,[C][D7][G]
For [Dm]lo, his [F]doom is [C]sure;[A][Dm][E]
One [Am]little [G7]word shall [C]fell him.[Dm][G7][C]

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.content:
That [C]word a[G]bove all [Am]earthly [D7]pow'rs,
No [Am]thanks to [Em]them, a[F]bid[C]eth;[Dm][G7][C]
The [G]Spirit [Am]and the [D7]gifts are [G]ours
Through [Am]Him who [Em]with us [F]sid[C]eth.[Dm][G7][C]
Let [Am]goods and [G]kindred [D7]go,
This [C]mortal [G7]life al[C]so;[F][E][Am]
The [E]body [Am]they may [G]kill,[C][D7][G]
God's [Dm]truth a[F]bideth [C]still;[A][Dm][E]
His [Am]kingdom [G7]is for[C]ever.[Dm][G7][C]

sections.label: Verse 1
Verse 2
Verse 3
sections.content:
No [D]more let sins and [A7]sorrows [D]grow,
Nor [G]thorns in[A7]fest the [D]ground;
He [D]comes to make His blessings flow
Far [D]as the curse is [A7]found,
Far [A7]as the curse is [D]found,
Far [D]as, far [G]as the [A7]curse is [D]found.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.content:
He [D]rules the world with [A7]truth and [D]grace,
And [G]makes the [A7]nations [D]prove
The [D]glories of His righteousness,
And [D]wonders of His [A7]love,
And [A7]wonders of His [D]love,
And [D]wonders, [G]wonders [A7]of His [D]love.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
[G]Silent night, holy night,
[D]All is calm, [D7]all is [G]bright.
[C]Round yon virgin [G]mother and child,
[C]Holy infant so [G]tender and mild,
[D]Sleep in [D7]heavenly [G]peace,[Em]
[G]Sleep in [D7]heavenly [G]peace.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
[G]Silent night, holy night,
[D]Shepherds quake [D7]at the [G]sight.
[C]Glories stream from [G]heaven afar,
[C]Heav'nly hosts sing [G]"Alleluia!"
[D]Christ the [D7]Savior is [G]born,[Em]
[G]Christ the [D7]Savior is [G]born.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
[G]Silent night, holy night,
[D]Son of God, [D7]love's pure [G]light.
[C]Radiant beams from [G]Thy holy face,
[C]With the dawn of re[G]deeming grace,
[D]Jesus, [D7]Lord, at Thy [G]birth,[Em]
[G]Jesus, [D7]Lord, at Thy [G]birth.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
O [Em]come, O come, Em[Am]manu[D]el,
And [G]ransom captive [C]Isra[Am]el,[Em]
That [Am]mourns in lonely [Em]exile [Bm]here,
Un[Em]til the Son of [Am]God ap[D]pear.[Em]

sections.label: Chorus
sections.content:
Re[D]joice! Re[G]joice! Em[C]man[Am]u[Em]el
Shall [Am]come to thee, O [Em]Isra[D]el.[Em]

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
O [Em]come, Thou Day-spring, [Am]come and [D]cheer
Our [G]spirits by Thine [C]advent [Am]here;[Em]
Dis[Am]perse the gloomy [Em]clouds of [Bm]night,
And [Em]death's dark shadows [Am]put to [D]flight.[Em]

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
O [Em]come, Thou Wisdom [Am]from on [D]high,
And [G]order all things, [C]far and [Am]nigh;[Em]
To [Am]us the path of [Em]knowledge [Bm]show,
And [Em]cause us in her [Am]ways to [D]go.[Em]

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.content:
O [Em]come, Desire of [Am]nations, [D]bind
All [G]peoples in one [C]heart and [Am]mind;[Em]
Bid [Am]envy, strife and [Em]quarrels [Bm]cease;
Fill [Em]the whole world with [Am]heaven's [D]peace.[Em]

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
[A]Rock of Ages, [D]cleft for [A]me,
Let me [E]hide myself in [A]Thee;
Let the [E]water and the [A]blood,
From Thy [E]wounded side which [A]flowed,
Be of [D]sin the double [A]cure,
Save from [E]wrath and make me [A]pure.

sections.label: Verse 2
Verse 3
Verse 4
sections.content:
[A]While I draw this [D]fleeting [A]breath,
When mine [E]eyes shall close in [A]death,
When I [E]rise to worlds un[A]known,
And be[E]hold Thee on Thy [A]throne,
Rock of [D]Ages, cleft for [A]me,
Let me [E]hide myself in [A]Thee.

sections.label: Verse 1
Chorus
sections.content:
[C]Yes, Jesus [F]loves me!
[C]Yes, Jesus [G]loves me!
[C]Yes, Jesus [F]loves me!
The [C]Bible [G7]tells me [C]so.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
[C]Jesus loves me! Loves me still,
[F]Though I'm very [C]weak and ill;
That I [C]might from sin be free,
[F]Bled and died up[C]on the [G7]tree.[C]

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
[C]Jesus loves me! He who died
[F]Heaven's gate to [C]open wide;
He will [C]wash away my sin,
[F]Let His little [C]child come [G7]in.[C]

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.content:
[C]Jesus loves me! He will stay
[F]Close beside me [C]all the way;
Thou hast [C]bled and died for me,
[F]I will hence[C]forth live for [G7]Thee.[C]

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
[G]Nearer, my God, to [D7]Thee,
[Em]Nearer to [C]Thee![G][D]
E'en [G]though it be a [C]cross
That [G]raiseth [D7]me,[G]
[G]Still all my song shall [C]be,
[G]Nearer, my [D7]God, to [Em]Thee,[C]
[G]Nearer, my [D7]God, to [G]Thee,
Nearer to [D7]Thee![G]

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
[G]Though like the wander[D7]er,
[Em]The sun gone [C]down,[G][D]
[G]Darkness be over [C]me,
My [G]rest a [D7]stone;[G]
[G]Yet in my dreams I'd [C]be
[G]Nearer, my [D7]God, to [Em]Thee,[C]
[G]Nearer, my [D7]God, to [G]Thee,
Nearer to [D7]Thee![G]

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
[G]There let the way ap[D7]pear,
[Em]Steps unto [C]heav'n;[G][D]
[G]All that Thou sendest [C]me,
In [G]mercy [D7]giv'n;[G]
[G]Angels to beckon [C]me
[G]Nearer, my [D7]God, to [Em]Thee,[C]
[G]Nearer, my [D7]God, to [G]Thee,
Nearer to [D7]Thee![G]

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.content:
[G]Then, with my waking [D7]thoughts
[Em]Bright with Thy [C]praise,[G][D]
[G]Out of my stony [C]griefs
[G]Bethel I'll [D7]raise;[G]
[G]So by my woes to [C]be
[G]Nearer, my [D7]God, to [Em]Thee,[C]
[G]Nearer, my [D7]God, to [G]Thee,
Nearer to [D7]Thee![G]

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.content:
[G]Or if on joyful [D7]wing,
[Em]Cleaving the [C]sky,[G][D]
[G]Sun, moon, and stars for[C]got,
[G]Upward I [D7]fly,[G]
[G]Still all my song shall [C]be,
[G]Nearer, my [D7]God, to [Em]Thee,[C]
[G]Nearer, my [D7]God, to [G]Thee,
Nearer to [D7]Thee![G]

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
[D]Just as I am, with[A7]out one [D]plea,
But [A7]that Thy [G]blood was shed for [D]me,
And that Thou bidd'st me [G]come to Thee,
O [D]Lamb of [A]God, I [D]come, I come.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
[D]Just as I am, and [A7]waiting [D]not
To [A7]rid my [G]soul of one dark [D]blot,
To Thee whose blood can [G]cleanse each spot,
O [D]Lamb of [A]God, I [D]come, I come.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
[D]Just as I am, though [A7]tossed a[D]bout
With [A7]many a [G]conflict, many a [D]doubt,
Fightings and fears with[G]in, without,
O [D]Lamb of [A]God, I [D]come, I come.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.content:
[D]Just as I am, poor, [A7]wretched, [D]blind;
[A7]Sight, [G]riches, healing [D]of the mind,
Yea, all I need in [G]Thee to find,
O [D]Lamb of [A]God, I [D]come, I come.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.content:
[D]Just as I am, Thou [A7]wilt re[D]ceive,
Wilt [A7]welcome, [G]pardon, cleanse, re[D]lieve;
Because Thy promise [G]I believe,
O [D]Lamb of [A]God, I [D]come, I come.

sections.label: Verse 6
sections.content:
[D]Just as I am, Thy [A7]love un[D]known
Hath [A7]broken [G]every barrier [D]down;
Now, to be Thine, yea, [G]Thine alone,
O [D]Lamb of [A]God, I [D]come, I come.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
[G]Onward, Christian [D]soldiers,
[D7]Marching as to [G]war,
With the [Em]cross of [D]Jesus
[A7]Going on be[D]fore!
[G]Christ, the royal [C]Master,
[G]Leads against the [D]foe;
[G]Forward into [C]battle,
[G]See His [D7]banners [G]go!

sections.label: Chorus
sections.content:
[G]Onward, Christian [D]soldiers,
[D7]Marching as to [G]war,
With the [C]cross of [G]Jesus
[D]Going on be[G]fore!

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
[G]At the sign of [D]triumph
[D7]Satan's host doth [G]flee;
On, then, [Em]Christian [D]soldiers,
[A7]On to victo[D]ry!
[G]Hell's foundations [C]quiver
[G]At the shout of [D]praise;
[G]Brothers, lift your [C]voices,
[G]Loud your [D7]anthems [G]raise!

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
[G]Like a mighty [D]army
[D7]Moves the Church of [G]God;
Brothers, [Em]we are [D]treading
[A7]Where the saints have [D]trod.
[G]We are not di[C]vided,
[G]All one body [D]we,
[G]One in hope and [C]doctrine,
[G]One in [D7]chari[G]ty.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.content:
[G]Onward, then, ye [D]people,
[D7]Join our happy [G]throng,
Blend with [Em]ours your [D]voices
[A7]In the triumph [D]song;
[G]Glory, laud, and [C]honor,
[G]Unto Christ the [D]King;
[G]This through countless [C]ages
[G]Men and [D7]angels [G]sing.

songs.title: What A Friend We Have In Jesus
songs.author: Joseph M. Scriven

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
[F]What a friend we have in [Bb]Jesus,
[F]All our sins and griefs to [C]bear!
[F]What a privilege to [Bb]carry
[F]Everything to [C]God in [F]prayer!
[F]O what peace we often [Bb]forfeit,
[F]O what needless pain we [C]bear,
[F]All because we do not [Bb]carry
[F]Everything to [C]God in [F]prayer.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
[F]Have we trials and temp[Bb]tations?
[F]Is there trouble any[C]where?
[F]We should never be dis[Bb]couraged;
[F]Take it to the [C]Lord in [F]prayer.
[F]Can we find a friend so [Bb]faithful
[F]Who will all our sorrows [C]share?
[F]Jesus knows our every [Bb]weakness;
[F]Take it to the [C]Lord in [F]prayer.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
[F]Are we weak and heavy-[Bb]laden,
[F]Cumbered with a load of [C]care?
[F]Precious Savior, still our [Bb]refuge,
[F]Take it to the [C]Lord in [F]prayer.
[F]Do thy friends despise, for[Bb]sake thee?
[F]Take it to the Lord in [C]prayer!
[F]In His arms He'll take and [Bb]shield thee;
[F]Thou wilt find a [C]solace [F]there.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
[G]Blessed assurance, [C]Jesus is [G]mine!
O what a [D]foretaste of [Em]glory di[D]vine!
[G]Heir of sal[C]vation, [G]purchase of God,
[Am]Born of His [G]Spirit, [D]washed in His [G]blood.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.content:
[D]This is my [G]story, [C]this is my [G]song,
[D]Praising my [Em]Savior [A]all the day [D]long;
[G]This is my [C]story, [G]this is my song,
[Am]Praising my [G]Savior [D]all the day [G]long.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
[G]Perfect sub[C]mission, [G]perfect delight,
[D]Visions of [Em]rapture now [D]burst on my sight;
[G]Angels de[C]scending [G]bring from above
[Am]Echoes of [G]mercy, [D]whispers of [G]love.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
[G]Perfect sub[C]mission, [G]all is at rest;
[D]I in my [Em]Savior am [D]happy and blest,
[G]Watching and [C]waiting, [G]looking above,
[Am]Filled with His [G]goodness, [D]lost in His [G]love.

songs.title: It Is Well With My Soul
songs.author: Horatio G. Spafford

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
When [C]peace like a [Dm]river at[G]tendeth my [C]way,
When [C]sorrows like [F]sea billows [G]roll;
What[C]ever my [Am]lot, Thou hast [Dm]taught me to [G]say,
"It is [C]well, it is [G]well with my [C]soul."

sections.label: Chorus
sections.content:
[C]It is [G]well ([Am]it is well)
With my [F]soul (with my [C]soul),
[C]It is [F]well, it is [C]well [G]with my [C]soul.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
Though [C]Satan should [Dm]buffet, though [G]trials should [C]come,
Let [C]this blest as[F]surance con[G]trol,
That [C]Christ has re[Am]garded my [Dm]helpless es[G]tate,
And hath [C]shed His own [G]blood for my [C]soul.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
My [C]sin, O the [Dm]bliss of this [G]glorious [C]thought!
My [C]sin, not in [F]part, but the [G]whole,
Is [C]nailed to the [Am]cross, and I [Dm]bear it no [G]more;
Praise the [C]Lord, praise the [G]Lord, O my [C]soul!

sections.label: Verse 4
Verse 1
sections.content:
On a [G]hill far away stood an [G7]old rugged cross,
The [C]emblem of suff'ring and [G]shame;
And I [G]love that old cross where the [G7]dearest and best
For a [D]world of lost sinners was [D7]slain.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.content:
So I'll [G]cherish the [G7]old rugged [C]cross,[Cm]
Till my [G]trophies at last I lay [D]down;[D7]
I will [G]cling to the [G7]old rugged [C]cross,[Cm]
And ex[G]change it some [D7]day for a [G]crown.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
O that [G]old rugged cross, so de[G7]spised by the world,
Has a [C]wondrous attraction for [G]me;
For the [G]dear Lamb of God left His [G7]glory above
To [D]bear it to dark Calva[D7]ry.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
In the [G]old rugged cross, stained with [G7]blood so divine,
A [C]wondrous beauty I [G]see;
For 'twas [G]on that old cross Jesus [G7]suffered and died,
To [D]pardon and sanctify [D7]me.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.content:
To the [G]old rugged cross I will [G7]ever be true,
Its [C]shame and reproach gladly [G]bear;
Then He'll [G]call me some day to my [G7]home far away,
Where His [D]glory forever I'll [D7]share.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
I [G]come to the garden a[D]lone,
While the [D7]dew is still on the [G]roses;
And the [G]voice I hear, falling [Em]on my ear,
The [A7]Son of God dis[D]closes.[D7]

sections.label: Chorus
sections.content:
And He [D]walks with me, and He [D7]talks with me,
And He [G]tells me I am His [G7]own;
And the [C]joy we share as we [Cm]tarry there,
[G]None other has [D7]ever [G]known.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
He [G]speaks, and the sound of His [D]voice
Is so [D7]sweet the birds hush their [G]singing,
And the [G]melody that He [Em]gave to me
With[A7]in my heart is [D]ringing.[D7]

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
I'd [G]stay in the garden with [D]Him,
Though the [D7]night around me be [G]falling,
But He [G]bids me go; through the [Em]voice of woe
His [A7]voice to me is [D]calling.[D7]

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.content:
[D]Be Thou my [G]Vision, O [D]Lord of my [A]heart;
[G]Naught be all [A]else to me, [G]save that Thou [D]art.
[G]Thou my best [D]thought, by [Bm]day or by [G]night,[A]
[Bm]Waking or [G]sleeping, Thy [A]presence my [D]light.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.content:
[D]Be Thou my [G]Wisdom, and [D]Thou my true [A]Word;
[G]I ever [A]with Thee and [G]Thou with [D]me, Lord.
[G]Thou my great [D]Father, I [Bm]Thy true [G]son;[A]
[Bm]Thou in me [G]dwelling, and [A]I with Thee [D]one.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.content:
[D]Riches I [G]heed not, nor [D]man's empty [A]praise,
[G]Thou mine in[A]heritance, [G]now and [D]always.
[G]Thou and Thou [D]only, [Bm]first in my [G]heart,[A]
[Bm]High King of [G]Heaven, my [A]treasure Thou [D]art.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.content:
[D]High King of [G]Heaven, my [D]victory [A]won,
[G]May I reach [A]heaven's joys, O [G]bright heav'n's [D]Sun!
[G]Heart of my [D]own heart, what[Bm]ever be[G]fall,[A]
[Bm]Still be my [G]Vision, O [A]Ruler of [D]all.
